name: rust-rocksdb
description: High-performance Rust bindings for RocksDB, a proven LSM-tree based key-value store optimized for write-heavy workloads

description.value: High-performance Rust bindings for RocksDB, a proven LSM-tree based key-value store optimized for write-heavy workloads
description.comment: Wraps Facebook's battle-tested RocksDB C++ library, used in production at Facebook, Yahoo, LinkedIn, and many others

implementation.comment: Rust wrapper around C++ RocksDB via FFI (librocksdb-sys)

schema-model.comment: Raw byte-oriented key-value store with optional column families for namespacing

query-language.comment: Supports range scans via iterators and prefix seeks for multimap-like queries

transactions.comment: Supports both pessimistic (TransactionDB) and optimistic transactions with ACID guarantees

range-queries.comment: Native support for ordered iteration with automatic readahead and prefix seek optimization

secondary-indexes.comment: No built-in secondary indexes; requires manual implementation or third-party libraries like mokuroku

in-memory-mode.comment: Can operate as in-memory database with WAL on persistent storage for durability

storage-architecture.comment: Multi-level LSM-tree with memtable, WAL, and tiered SSTables. Optimized for write-heavy workloads and SSDs

compression.comment: Supports Snappy, LZ4, Zstd, Zlib, and Bzip2 compression via feature flags (all enabled by default)

encryption-at-rest.comment: File-based or block-based encryption via modular SSTable encryption support, requires external key management

memory-mapped-io.comment: Optional mmap support via allow_mmap_reads/allow_mmap_writes options; not recommended for block devices due to performance issues

max-db-size.comment: No hard size limit; can cap total disk space via SstFileManager API. Proven at multi-petabyte scale

pure-rust.comment: Requires C++ RocksDB library and Clang/LLVM for building; not pure Rust

async-support.comment: Synchronous API only; use tokio::task::spawn_blocking() for async runtimes to avoid blocking

api-safety.comment: Safe Rust API wrapping FFI calls; unsafe code hidden behind abstraction layer with automatic resource cleanup

idiomatic-api.comment: Reasonably idiomatic with RAII patterns and type-based threading modes, but byte-oriented interface and complex options structs reflect C++ origins

type-safety.comment: Raw byte slice API (&[u8]); no built-in type safety for keys/values

serde-integration.comment: No native serde support; optional 'serde1' feature for internal types only. Third-party wrappers like rocksbin-db provide automatic serialization

no-std.comment: Requires std and filesystem access; not no_std compatible

wasm-compatible.comment: Not WASM-compatible due to C++ dependencies, file I/O, and threading requirements

acid-compliant.comment: Full ACID guarantees with WriteBatch atomicity, configurable isolation levels (snapshot or current), and WAL durability

crash-recovery.comment: Automatic recovery via WAL replay; guarantees no data loss with WriteOptions.sync=true, recovers committed data otherwise

concurrent-readers.comment: Lock-free concurrent reads within a single process via multiversion concurrency control (MVCC)

concurrent-writers.comment: Multiple threads can write concurrently; write requests may be batched for efficiency

multi-process.comment: Single process per database via file locking (db/LOCK); read-only secondary instances or RPC proxy required for multi-process access

write-ahead-log.comment: WAL for durability with configurable directory, TTL, size limits, and manual/auto flush modes

maturity.comment: Production-proven at web scale (Facebook, LinkedIn, Yahoo); RocksDB itself has 10+ years of development, Rust bindings actively maintained since 2014

linux.comment: Full support including io-uring feature for optimized async I/O

windows.comment: Supports both /MT and /MD runtime libraries via mt_static feature

ios.comment: Possible but challenging; requires manual cross-compilation. Third-party Kotlin Multiplatform wrappers provide easier integration

android.comment: Supported but requires NDK compilation. Third-party rocksdb-android library provides pre-built binaries for arm64-v8a, armeabi-v7a, x86, x86_64

embedded.comment: Not suitable for embedded systems; requires std, filesystem, and substantial resources

use-cases.comment: Ideal for write-heavy workloads: streaming state stores (Kafka Streams, Flink), metadata indexing, time-series/logs, blockchain ledgers, AI model serving caches

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads as of December 2024; librocksdb-sys has 35M+ downloads

last-release.comment: Version 0.24.0 with RocksDB v10.4.2, added AIX support, GCC 15 support, WriteBatch::put_log_data

maintenance-status.comment: Active development with 202 contributors, regular updates tracking upstream RocksDB releases, multiple issues resolved in 2025

documentation-quality.comment: Good API docs with basic examples; relies heavily on upstream RocksDB wiki for concepts. Some advanced features need better Rust-specific documentation

bus-factor.comment: Community-maintained with documented maintainership process requiring consensus; 202 total contributors across project history

hot-backup.comment: BackupEngine API creates consistent backups while database is running with checksum verification. Checkpoint API creates point-in-time snapshots using hard links.

incremental-backup.comment: BackupEngine creates incremental backups by default - SST files are immutable, so unchanged files are shared between backup snapshots.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: No restore to arbitrary timestamp. WAL 'PointInTimeRecovery' mode refers to crash recovery stopping at first corrupted record, not user-specified timestamp restore.

online-compaction.comment: Automatic background compaction runs continuously via configurable thread pools. Manual compaction via compact_range() is blocking but database remains operational.

repair-tools.comment: DBCommon::repair() method for programmatic recovery. Upstream ldb repair CLI tool available. Scans files, replays WAL, rebuilds manifest.

export-format.comment: No built-in JSON/CSV export. Native formats: SST files, hex dump via ldb, or ROCKDUMP format. Portable export requires custom iteration code.
